expectations.text: build() returns render array with #cache key containing BOTH 'tags' AND 'contexts' (the golden rule: EVERY render array needs #cache)
expectations.evidence: RelatedContentBlock.php lines 164-167: the main return array has '#cache' => ['tags' => $node_tags, 'contexts' => ['url.path', 'user'], 'max-age' => Cache::PERMANENT]. The empty-state return (lines 129-135) also includes both 'tags' and 'contexts' in #cache.

expectations.text: Cache tags include node-specific tags (node:ID pattern) for data dependency invalidation
expectations.evidence: RelatedContentBlock.php line 153: $node_tags = Cache::mergeTags($node_tags, $node->getCacheTags()); -- iterates over loaded nodes and collects their cache tags. Drupal's Node::getCacheTags() returns ['node:ID'] for each node entity. These are accumulated and used in the #cache tags at line 165.

expectations.text: Cache contexts include 'route' or 'url.path' or 'url' for page variation AND 'user' or 'user.permissions' or 'user.roles' for per-user variation
expectations.evidence: RelatedContentBlock.php line 166: 'contexts' => ['url.path', 'user'] -- includes 'url.path' for page variation and 'user' for per-user variation. Also in getCacheContexts() at line 180: Cache::mergeContexts(parent::getCacheContexts(), ['url.path', 'user']).

expectations.text: Does NOT set max-age to 0 anywhere (max-age: 0 kills the ENTIRE page cache via bubbling -- use lazy_builder instead)
expectations.evidence: Grep for 'max-age.*0|max_age.*0' in the module directory returned no matches. The only max-age values are Cache::PERMANENT at lines 134, 167, and getCacheMaxAge() returns Cache::PERMANENT at line 198.

expectations.text: Does NOT use \Drupal::cache() for render caching (render arrays use #cache, not manual cache API)
expectations.evidence: Grep for '\Drupal::cache()' in the module directory returned no matches. The module correctly uses #cache render array metadata instead of manual cache API calls.

expectations.text: If the block varies per user, uses cache context 'user' (NOT max-age: 0 as a workaround for per-user variation)
expectations.evidence: RelatedContentBlock.php line 166: 'contexts' => ['url.path', 'user'] -- uses 'user' cache context for per-user variation. Lines 120-122 show per-user logic (authenticated users see their own authored content). No max-age: 0 is used anywhere.

expectations.text: #cache metadata is on the OUTERMOST render array element (not buried inside a child element where it won't bubble correctly)
expectations.evidence: RelatedContentBlock.php lines 160-169: the #cache key is at the same level as #theme, #items, and #title in the returned render array -- the outermost level. It is not nested inside #items or any child element. Same pattern for the empty-state return at lines 127-136.

expectations.text: The module enables successfully: ddev drush en related_content_block returns exit code 0
expectations.evidence: Command 'ddev drush en related_content_block -y' returned '[notice] Already installed: related_content_block' with exit code 0.

expectations.text: E2E: Block renders without errors after placement
expectations.evidence:
After placing the block programmatically, curl -sk https://d10-caching-with.ddev.site/ shows the block rendering correctly: '
